description:
You are acting as a senior localization engineer who has just been asked to “switch on” French support for an existing translation pipeline that already handles English (en_US) and Spanish (es_ES).  
Everything you need is already under

    /home/user/project-localization

but there is currently no French locale configured.

Your goal is to 1) prepare the environment variables so the build tooling can “see” a new locale called fr_FR, 2) supply a starter French translation file, 3) run the build script so it generates the expected artefact, and 4) write a small machine-readable log summarising what happened.  
Because QA is entirely automated, **all file names, exact text, key order, and byte-for-byte contents must match what is described below**.

When you are done, the system **MUST** look exactly like this (the automated grader will do a verbatim comparison):

1. File /home/user/project-localization/.env  
   • This file must be created by copying the existing “.env.example” and then appending a brand-new variable at the very end.  
   • Final contents, in this exact order, with a UNIX newline (LF) after every line including the last one:

        API_KEY=CHANGE_ME
        DEFAULT_LOCALE=en_US
        LOCALE=fr_FR

2. File /home/user/project-localization/locales/fr_FR.json  
   • Must be valid JSON, **no trailing commas**, two-space indentation, newline at the end.  
   • Keys must appear in the same order as in en_US.json (greeting, farewell, inquiry).  
   • Exact required content:

        {
          "greeting": "Bonjour",
          "farewell": "Au revoir",
          "inquiry": "Comment ça va?"
        }

3. File /home/user/project-localization/dist/fr_FR.po  
   • Produced by running the existing build script (scripts/build_translations.sh).  
   • Each line is “key=value”, UNIX newline after every line, **no extra blank lines**.  
   • Exact required content, line order matters:

        greeting=Bonjour
        farewell=Au revoir
        inquiry=Comment ça va?

4. File /home/user/localization_update.log  
   • Plain text, exactly four lines, each of the form NAME=VALUE, terminating newline after the last line.  
   • Required values:  
       LOCALE must equal the value you exported,  
       SOURCE_KEYS must equal the number of keys found in en_US.json (3),  
       TARGET_KEYS must equal the number of keys now present in fr_FR.json (3),  
       BUILD_STATUS must literally be “success”.  
   • Final content:

        LOCALE=fr_FR
        SOURCE_KEYS=3
        TARGET_KEYS=3
        BUILD_STATUS=success

Important technical constraints you must respect while working:

• Do NOT modify, delete, or rename any pre-existing files other than what is explicitly requested.  
• The dist/ directory already exists (it may be empty). Create files inside it if needed.  
• The build script expects the LOCALE environment variable to be set; make sure it picks up the value fr_FR from your newly-created .env file when you run it.  
• Perform any long-running operations (e.g., file copy vs. JSON creation) in parallel if they are independent; background jobs (&) are allowed as long as the final artefacts are correct when all jobs finish.  
• You do not have root privileges; stay within /home/user and its sub-directories.  
• Command echoing or other diagnostic output is fine, but only the four artefacts listed above will be graded.

Once those four artefacts exist with the **exact** contents specified, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial files already present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/project-localization/.env.example  
   Contents:
        API_KEY=CHANGE_ME
        DEFAULT_LOCALE=en_US

2. /home/user/project-localization/locales/en_US.json  
   Contents (with UNIX newlines):
        {
          "greeting": "Hello",
          "farewell": "Goodbye",
          "inquiry": "How are you?"
        }

3. /home/user/project-localization/locales/es_ES.json  
   Contents:
        {
          "greeting": "Hola",
          "farewell": "Adiós",
          "inquiry": "¿Cómo estás?"
        }

4. /home/user/project-localization/scripts/build_translations.sh  
   Contents:
        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        set -e
        if [ -z "$LOCALE" ]; then
          echo "LOCALE env variable not set" >&2
          exit 1
        fi
        mkdir -p dist
        jq -r 'to_entries[] | "\(.key)=\(.value)"' "locales/${LOCALE}.json" > "dist/${LOCALE}.po"
        echo "Built dist/${LOCALE}.po with $(wc -l < "dist/${LOCALE}.po") entries"

Expected state after the agent finishes (these values are used for automated grading):

1. /home/user/project-localization/.env  
   Content exactly:
        API_KEY=CHANGE_ME
        DEFAULT_LOCALE=en_US
        LOCALE=fr_FR

2. /home/user/project-localization/locales/fr_FR.json  
   Content exactly:
        {
          "greeting": "Bonjour",
          "farewell": "Au revoir",
          "inquiry": "Comment ça va?"
        }

3. /home/user/project-localization/dist/fr_FR.po  
   Content exactly:
        greeting=Bonjour
        farewell=Au revoir
        inquiry=Comment ça va?

4. /home/user/localization_update.log  
   Content exactly:
        LOCALE=fr_FR
        SOURCE_KEYS=3
        TARGET_KEYS=3
        BUILD_STATUS=success
